summary: Frontend integration testing for read-only mode completed successfully. All 7 features tested and working correctly: ReadOnlyBanner shows at top of page when subscription is expired, banner includes 'Subscribe Now' button linking to /settings?tab=subscription, Dashboard loads and displays all data in read-only mode, Minutes page loads data in read-only mode, Distributions page loads data in read-only mode, write operations blocked with 403 error when subscription expired, and banner does NOT appear when subscription is active.

ui_bugs.issue: Error toast shows 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of proper subscription error message

passed_tests: ReadOnlyBanner appears at top of Dashboard when subscription is expired
ReadOnlyBanner has amber/warning color scheme (bg-amber-50 border-amber-200)
ReadOnlyBanner displays 'You can view all your data, but creating or editing is disabled until you subscribe.' message
ReadOnlyBanner includes 'Subscribe Now' button with data-testid='subscribe-now-btn'
ReadOnlyBanner appears on Minutes page when subscription expired
ReadOnlyBanner appears on Distributions page when subscription expired
Dashboard data loads correctly in read-only mode (governance health score, quick actions, recent activity)
Minutes data loads correctly in read-only mode (6 minutes entries visible)
Distributions data loads correctly in read-only mode (3 distribution records, $25,500 total)
GET /api/distributions returns 200 and data when subscription expired
GET /api/minutes returns 200 and data when subscription expired
GET /api/dashboard returns 200 and data when subscription expired
POST /api/distributions returns 403 with 'subscription is inactive' error when subscription expired
ReadOnlyBanner does NOT appear when subscription is active
SubscriptionGate correctly renders children with ReadOnlyBanner wrapper when expired
action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW PRIORITY: Improve error handling in fetchWithAuth to properly parse 403 subscription errors and show user-friendly toast message instead of 'body stream already read' error
critical_code_review_comments: ReadOnlyBanner component properly uses useAuth hook to get isReadOnly and subscriptionExpired state
SubscriptionGate correctly wraps children with ReadOnlyBanner at top when subscription is expired
LoginPage correctly calls loadSubscriptionState() after successful login to set read-only state
Banner appearance is correct with amber color scheme and clear messaging
data-testid attributes present on key elements (read-only-banner, subscribe-now-btn)

success_rate.backend: 100% - All GET requests work, POST properly blocked with 403
success_rate.frontend: 95% - All features work, minor toast error handling issue

seed_data_creation: Temporarily modified subscription trial_end_date to '2025-01-01' and status to 'expired' for testing, then restored to original values
context_for_next_testing_agent: Read-only mode frontend integration fully tested. ReadOnlyBanner component works correctly - appears at top of pages when subscription is expired, shows amber warning styling, includes Subscribe Now button linking to /settings?tab=subscription. All pages (Dashboard, Minutes, Distributions) load and display data in read-only mode. Write operations are blocked by backend returning 403 with appropriate error message. Banner does NOT appear when subscription is active. Minor issue: error toast shows generic JSON error instead of subscription message, but functionality is correct.
